mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on a continuous basis regardless of level.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20-day SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declining-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals a labor market inflection consistent with the Sahm rule, triggering risk-off equity flows and depressing S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as recession probability re-prices.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the 0.3pp trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals durable economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this synergy since retail sales can rise on credit-driven consumption while housing starts independently reflect capex-like commitments.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to outperform either single-series signal in Rank IC by any margin over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects improving manufacturing throughput and supply-side momentum that markets price quickly, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as a short-horizon continuation effect.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign concordance rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when the federal funds rate is rising while CPI inflation remains elevated above trend, markets interpret continued hawkish policy as a threat to valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; outside tightening regimes this joint signal has no reliable directional effect because rate and inflation co-movement can reflect healthy reflation instead.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3+ consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
failure_condition: Conditional on the joint trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals an abrupt labor demand contraction that triggers negative revisions to earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the PAYEMS decline trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating real GDP growth signals waning aggregate demand momentum that has historically preceded equity market corrections over the subsequent quarter, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in early or mid-cycle regimes the same deceleration reflects normal moderation and carries no reliable directional signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth decelerates for 2+ consecutive quarters
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes, 60-day forward SPX returns following the deceleration trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals an emerging supply-and-labor-side downturn that markets have not yet fully priced, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; neither series alone reliably predicts this short-horizon move since single-month INDPRO or PAYEMS declines are often noise, but their joint occurrence indicates a coordinated real-economy shock.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM < 0 AND PAYEMS MoM < 0 in the same month
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline jointly, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint-trigger Rank IC does not exceed either single-series Rank IC by any measurable margin.
